name: When Data Imbalance Helps: Robust Generalization Through Shortcut Saturation

description: arXiv:2607.10116v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We study robust generalization under spurious correlations: tasks where a shortcut feature is correlated with the true label in training but anti-correlated in an adversarial held-out split. Varying the spurious ratio $r$ (the fraction of training examples where shortcut = true label) and model capacity, we find a counterintuitive result: data i...
author: Cheng-Ting Chou, Duc Binh Hoang
